canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Fast-DetectGPT is consistently described as a procedure for determining whether a text is generated by a large language model. The method's mechanism is based on measuring probability curvature, referred to as either "conditional probability curvature" or "log-probability curvature" in different sources. One paper characterizes it as a "training-free" method, while another notes it is an "open-source" tool. Its application is framed in multiple ways; for instance, it is employed as a "safeguard for academic integrity" to classify machine-generated paper submissions. Other work uses it as a "representative training-free" baseline for comparison against other detection methods. In at least one evaluation, Fast-DetectGPT is reported to "struggle" in its detection performance when compared to other methods and human evaluators.
papers: CanLLMs Reason About Program Semantics? A Comprehensive Evaluation ofLLMs on Formal Specification Inference
Enhancing Interpretable Image Classification ThroughLLMAgents and Conditional Concept Bottleneck Models
Pattern Recognition or Medical Knowledge? The Problem with Multiple-Choice Questions in Medicine
When to Speak, When to Abstain: Contrastive Decoding with Abstention
E2LLM: Encoder Elongated Large Language Models for Long-Context Understanding and Reasoning
MUZO: Leveraging Multiple Queries and Momentum for Zeroth-Order Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models
Spontaneous Giving and Calculated Greed in Language Models
ThinkTuning: Instilling Cognitive Reflections without Distillation
CycleResearcher: Improving Automated Research via Automated Review
Deep Kernel Relative Test for Machine-generated Text Detection
Glimpse: Enabling White-Box Methods to Use Proprietary Models for Zero-Shot LLM-Generated Text Detection
Humanizing the Machine: Proxy Attacks to Mislead LLM Detectors
Training-free LLM-generated Text Detection by Mining Token Probability Sequences

definitions.text: A named procedure used to determine if a text is LLM-generated by measuring its conditional probability curvature against a threshold, employed as a safeguard for academic integrity.
definitions.paper: CycleResearcher: Improving Automated Research via Automated Review
